title: The Ghost in the Production Pipeline
subtitle: Why AI Agents Require Continuous Behavioral Evaluation and Runtime Governance
excerpt: As platforms shift from deterministic code to autonomous agentic behaviors, traditional APM and QA collapse. Continuous evaluation and runtime governance are the guardrails required to protect product integrity and the P&L.
imageAlt: The Agentic Governance Matrix comparing Continuous Evaluation and Runtime Governance

title: The Myth of the Functional Silo
subtitle: Why Modern Tech Leadership Requires Total Convergence of Product, Data, and Analytics
excerpt: Forcing product, data, and analytics into separate kingdoms is a structural liability. Total convergence is the commercial execution engine behind modern DaaS and decision-support platforms.
imageAlt: The Siloed Architecture Breakdown — product, data, and analytics fracture patterns

title: The Death of the 2-Week Sprint
subtitle: Overhauling Product Governance for Non-Deterministic Software
excerpt: Legacy Scrum sprints break when shipping agentic AI and data platforms. Contract-driven specs, trace audits, and consumption-aligned P&L replace the feature factory.
imageAlt: The SaaS Product Governance Shift — old playbook vs. agentic playbook

title: Automating Operational Risk
subtitle: Establishing Deterministic Data Guardrails for Autonomous Agents
excerpt: Bad data no longer causes bad text—it causes bad actions. MCPs, ingestion-filter layers, and runtime circuit breakers are the guardrails that keep autonomous agents from automating systemic operational risk.
imageAlt: Agentic Data Governance Layer — MCPs, ingestion filters, and circuit breakers

title: The Value of Data Compression
subtitle: Signals, Actions, and the Architecture of Regulatory Defensibility
excerpt: Data volume is a vanity metric. Enterprise value lies in compression ratio — distilling raw signals into defensible actions with full lineage, audit trails, and human-in-the-loop orchestration.
imageAlt: Token FinOps Monetization Engine — compression, consumption, and defensibility
